channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Two nabbed as police recover stolen N7.5m Adamawa vehicle
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Adamawa police recover a N7.5m stolen Toyota Hilux and arrest two suspects for criminal conspiracy and receiving stolen property.
Read More: https://punchng.com/two-nabbed-as-police-recover-stolen-n7-5m-adamawa-vehicle/

item.title: Court reserves judgment on Pantami’s emergence as PDP gov candidate
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Gombe Federal High Court reserves judgment on suits challenging Isa Pantami’s emergence as PDP governorship candidate for the 2027 election. Learn more abo
Read More: https://punchng.com/court-reserves-judgment-on-pantamis-emergence-as-pdp-gov-candidate/

item.title: Lagos blames heavy rainfall for worsening road conditions
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Lagos State Public Works Corporation attributes worsening Lagos roads to heavy rainfall, assuring residents of ongoing efforts to repair damaged infrastruc
Read More: https://punchng.com/lagos-blames-heavy-rainfall-for-worsening-road-conditions/

item.title: 2027: Tinubu presents Shettima APC VP nomination certificate
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
President Tinubu officially presents VP Kashim Shettima with his APC vice-presidential nomination certificate for the 2027 general election.
Read More: https://punchng.com/2027-tinubu-presents-shettima-apc-vp-nomination-certificate/

item.title: FULL LIST: Messi tops golden boot, FIFA power rankings ahead of W’Cup final
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Lionel Messi leads the Golden Boot race and FIFA’s Creativity Power Rankings ahead of the 2026 World Cup final. Get the full list of standings here.
Read More: https://punchng.com/full-list-messi-tops-golden-boot-fifa-power-rankings-ahead-of-wcup-final/

item.title: No regret missing out on 2026 World Cup – Iwobi
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Super Eagles midfielder Alex Iwobi discusses his 100th cap, missing the 2026 World Cup, Okocha’s influence, and his most memorable goal for Nigeria.
Read More: https://punchng.com/no-regret-missing-out-on-2026-world-cup-iwobi/

item.title: The Open LIVE! Leader Suber among early starters on day two
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football

item.title: FIFA names officials for Spain, Argentina World Cup final
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
FIFA announces Slavko Vinčić will referee the 2026 World Cup Final between Spain and Argentina. Get details on officials and match info.
Read More: https://punchng.com/fifa-names-officials-for-spain-argentina-world-cup-final/

item.title: Family begs FG to recover slain Oyo teacher’s remains
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Family of Deacon John Olaleye, a teacher killed during the Oriire abduction, appeals to FG and Governor Makinde to recover his remains for burial.
Read More: https://punchng.com/family-begs-fg-to-recover-slain-oyo-teachers-remains/

item.title: England vs Argentina: A one-sided rugby rivalry - will Pumas bite back?
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Fresh from the drama of Wednesday's FIFA World Cup semi-final between England and Argentina, the nations meet again on Saturday as part of rugby's Nations Championship in Santiago del Estero.

item.title: Today on Sky Sports Racing: Arapaho Gold chases Newbury hat-trick
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Newbury and Wolverhampton are the venues for Friday's live action on Sky Sports Racing.

item.title: ‘I used to inherit my friends’ beefs’ – Bisola Aiyeola reveals
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Popular reality TV star and actress, Bisola Aiyeola has opened up about how uncontrolled loyalty made her took sides and got involved in fights that weren’t hers. Speaking in a recent episode of the Dear Ife podcast, the movie star said she used to “inherit” her loved ones, friends

item.title: ‘It’s huge mistake’ – Lawyer raises concern over nomination of ex-FUOYE VC, Fasina as ambassador
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Human rights lawyer, Frank Tietie, has raised the alarm over the nomination and confirmation of former Vice-Chancellor of the Federal University, Oye-Ekiti, Professor Abayomi Fasina, as a non-career ambassador designate. Speaking during an interview on Arise Television on Thursday, Tietie said it wa

item.title: Obidient Movement accuses EFCC of targeting Obi’s supporters in naira abuse probe
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Obidient Movement has alleged that the Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC) is using its investigation into alleged naira abuse to target supporters of its presidential candidate, Peter Obi. The post Obidient Movement accuses EFCC of targeting Obi’s supporters in naira abuse pro

item.title: No N210trn missing from NNPC accounts, ex-NAPIMS GM tells senate committee
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Henry Umoru ABUJA – FORMER Group General Manager (GGM) of the National Petroleum Investment Management Services (NAPIMS), Bala Wunti, has disclosed that there was no evidence in the 2023 audited financial statements of the Nigerian National Petroleum Company (NNPC) Limited to support claims that

item.title: China hits out at British Steel nationalisation
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: The UK government said taking the firm into public hands would safeguard "a vital national capability".

item.title: Cincinnati Open: Alcaraz Set To Return To ATP Tour
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Spanish tennis star Carlos Alcaraz is targeting a competitive return at the Cincinnati Open as he prepares to defend his US Open title. Sidelined since April by a right wrist injury that forced him to miss the French Open and Wimbledon, the Spaniard is now cleared and ramping up his training intensi

item.title: 2027: INEC to conduct mock presidential poll
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: THE Independent National Electoral Commission, INEC, yesterday, disclosed that it is considering conducting a comprehensive audit of all its electoral technology systems... The post 2027: INEC to conduct mock presidential poll appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Bauchi guber: Bala Mohammed’s ex-campaign DG, Faruk Mustapha, emerges APC’s Abubakar running mate
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The All Progressives Congress, APC, in Bauchi State has picked former Commissioner for Special Duties and Rural Development, Hon. Faruk Mustapha, as the running mate to its 2027 governorship candidate, Barr Mohammed Abdullahi Abubakar, SAN. The choice of Mustapha ends weeks of speculation over who w

item.title: Effort, spirit and moments - but do England lack all-round quality?
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: As England reflect on their World Cup semi-final defeat by Argentina, BBC Sport's Phil McNulty looks at what Thomas Tuchel's team were lacking.

item.title: Two bandits killed, two kidnap victims rescued in Kwara
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: A security team comprising operatives of the Department of State Services, DSS, and forest guards on Thursday rescued two kidnap victims in a forest between Oba Isin/Owa Onire area of isin/ Ifelodun Local Government Areas of Kwara State. The rescued victims are 16-year-old Fatima Azeez and

item.title: CPPE worried over renewed acceleration in food inflation
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: •calls for sustained moderation in prices The Centre for Promotion of Private Enterprise (CPPE) has expressed concerns over the renewed acceleration in food inflation as reported by the National Bureau of Statistics (NBS) in its recently released June Inflation Report. This centre, therefore, called

item.title: Tinubu approves Lagos-Ibadan road reconstruction, legacy highway expansion
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
President Tinubu approves major road projects, including Lagos-Ibadan Expressway reconstruction and Fourth Legacy Highway expansion. Learn more about Niger
Read More: https://punchng.com/tinubu-approves-lagos-ibadan-road-reconstruction-legacy-highway-expansion/

item.title: Rising petrol prices tighten margins for fuel importers
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Nigerian fuel importers face tighter margins due to rising international petrol prices, higher freight costs, and Dangote Refinery’s pricing strategy. Lear
Read More: https://punchng.com/rising-petrol-prices-tighten-margins-for-fuel-importers/

item.title: Don’t get married if you don’t have money – Jarvis advises singles
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Nigerian TikTok creator, Amadou Elizabeth Aminata, popularly known as Jarvis, has shared marital advice ahead of her white wedding with fellow TikTok star, Peller. The TikTok influencer urged Nigerian youths not to allow themselves be pressured into marriage until they are financially stable.&#

item.title: Shettima’s Final Test
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Vice President Kashim Shettima cannot be blamed for having doubts about whether President Bola Ahmed Tinubu would renominate him as his running mate for a second term. As governor of Lagos State for eight years, Tinubu used three deputies: Kofoworola Bucknor-Akerele, Femi Pedro, and Abiodun Ogunleye

item.title: I’m freezing my eggs to keep my options open for future – BBNaija star, Tolanibaj
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Former Big Brother Naija housemate and disc jockey, Tolanibaj, has revealed that she is taking proactive steps toward preserving her fertility. The 32-year-old explained that she is taking the initiative to keep her options open, adding that she would rather have options than regrets in the future.

item.title: ‘Davido is an angel’: Zubby Michael recalls life-changing experience
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Zubby also disclosed that the singer gave him his first private jet experience, a gesture he said he will never forget.

item.title: Nigerian Newspapers: Major stories on Friday
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Good morning to readers. Daily Sun brings you the major stories from Nigerian Newspapers on Friday morning. The post Nigerian Newspapers: Major stories on Friday appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: NCC reviews MVNO business rules to deepen competition, expand telecom access
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Nigerian Communications Commission (NCC) has initiated a review of the Mobile Virtual Network Operators, MVNO, Business Rules to enhance competition and broaden access to telecommunications services within the nation. Dr. Aminu Maida, the Executive Vice-Chairman, EVC, of the NCC, made this announcem

item.title: Peller threatens to stop patronising actors, musicians if they miss his wedding
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Nigerian TikTok creator, Hamzat Habeeb Adelaja, popularly known as Peller, has threatened to cut ties and stop patronising any celebrity who fails to attend his upcoming white wedding with his wife, fellow TikTok star, Amadou Elizabeth Aminata, known as Jarvis.  Speaking in a recent video broad

item.title: Net FX Reserves Jumped 1,233% To $40bn, Could Provide 10-month Import Cover – CBN
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The governor of the Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN), Olayemi Cardoso, has disclosed that Nigeria’s net foreign exchange (FX) reserves have surged by more than 1,233 per cent from about $3 billion to over $40 billion, attributing the remarkable increase to the apex bank’s foreign exchange reforms aimed

item.title: ‘EFCC is finished, now symbol of joblessness, misplaced priorities’ – Sowore
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The presidential candidate of the African Action Congress, AAC, has lashed out at the Economic and Financial Crimes Commission, EFCC. In a post on his verified X handle on Thursday, Sowore said the anti-graft agency had become a symbol of joblessness and misplaced priorities. He was reacting to the

item.title: ‘I don’t know what it means to be broke’ – Eniola Badmus
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Nollywood actress and Special Assistant to the Speaker of the Nigerian House of Representatives on Social Events and Public Hearings, Eniola Badmus, has sparked reactions after boasting that she doesn’t know what it means to be broke. During an interview on the red carpet of the premiere

item.title: Investment Bank Wins Euromoney Award For Financing Solutions
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Iron Capital has been named Nigeria’s Best Investment Bank for Financing Solutions at the 2026 Euromoney Awards for Excellence. The company announced the recognition recently, saying the award acknowledges its work in transaction structuring and financing across the African financial market. C

item.title: Malawi: UK Announces Deep Cuts to Malawi Aid Funding
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [Nyasa Times] The UK government has announced plans to cut its international development funding to Malawi by 60% in 2026-27, with reductions reaching 90% by 2028-29 compared with 2025-26 levels.

item.title: Japan relaxes royal succession rules - but ban on female emperors remain
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: The new bill also allows the adoption of male distant relatives aged over 15 back into the imperial family.

item.title: African Leaders Unveil Plan To Boost Intra-African Livestock Trade
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: African leaders have launched a coordinated agenda aimed at transforming the continent’s pastoral livestock sector into a globally competitive industry by removing trade barriers, attracting private investment and creating a unified livestock market under the African Continental Free Trade Are

item.title: Alvarez Won’t Join Barcelona –Atletico Madrid President
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Atlético Madrid President Enrique Cerezo has firmly dismissed Barcelona’s interest in Julián Álvarez, stating that the Argentine forward is not for sale. Recall that Barcelona see Álvarez as a primary target to eventually succeed Robert Lewandowski. Read Also:World Cup 2026: I Am Disappointed In Mys

item.title: Texas floods ravage through southwestern areas of state
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: At least 2 people are dead, and over 200 were rescued in catastrophic floods in the Texas Hill Country region.

item.title: Sudan: Sudan Again Rejects Us Claims That Army Used Chemical Weapons
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [SNA] New York, July 16, 2026 (SUNA) - Sudan has rejected renewed claims by the United States representative before the UN Security Council regarding the Sudanese Armed Forces' alleged use of chemical weapons.

item.title: Stakeholders Back Ethical Governance, Sustainable Finance
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Stakeholders from Nigeria’s financial sector, development institutions and regulatory agencies have reaffirmed the need for ethical governance, sustainable finance and stronger public-private collaboration to build a resilient and inclusive African economy. The commitment was made at the 2026

item.title: Three Years On: Governor Bago Providing Affordable Housing, Others In Niger
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Niger State Government has acknowledged the necessity to expedite the completion of the 5-star Hotel in Minna, the state capital, as a necessary component of the Urban Renewal Programs of the administration. The state Governor, Farmer Umaru Mohammed Bago while marking his Third Anniversary in office

item.title: Olympic cyclist admits to driving despite ban after crash that killed wife
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Rohan Dennis pleaded guilty to driving while disqualified after police pulled him over last month.

item.title: Asake announces “In God We Trust” world tour
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: According to the ‘Lungu Boy’ crooner, the tour will begin with a gig at Afro Nation Portugal before moving to North America in August with performances in Chicago, Toronto, Brooklyn, Atlanta, Houston and Los Angeles.

item.title: Uganda: Kapchorwa Bus Tragedy - 23 Ndejje Pupils Feared Dead After School Tour Turns Fatal
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [New Light] Kapchorwa: At least 23 pupils of King David Junior School Ndejje are feared dead following a road accident in Kapchorwa District on Thursday evening, as the pupils travelled for a field trip whose exact destination remained unclear at the time of the crash.

item.title: Building Enduring Foundations: Nasarawa’s Bet On Education
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The World Bank puts it succinctly: education is foundational infrastructure for job creation. It is not charity. It is not political patronage. It is not merely another campaign promise. It is infrastructure—just like roads, electricity, water, and digital connectivity. It is the engine that moves s

item.title: Namibia: Repatriated Namibians Eye South Africa Return
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [Namibian] The government will assess future assistance requests on a case-by-case basis as repatriated citizens express hope of returning to South Africa.

item.title: Malawi: Malawi Parliament Committee Flags Funding Gaps At Public Universities, Calls for Increased Subventions
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [Nyasa Times] Malawi's Parliamentary Committee on Education, Science and Technology has called for increased government subventions to public universities, warning that persistent underfunding is undermining the quality of higher education across the country.

item.title: EFCC re-arraigns Tunde Ayeni for Alleged N15.6b fraud
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: In one of the charges, EFCC alleged that Ayeni, whilst being the Chairman, Board of Directors of the defunct Skye Bank Plc (now Polaris Bank Limited) on 20th November 2014 at Abuja committed criminal breach of trust by dishonestly transferring over N3 billion depositors' funds to Misa Limited's acco

item.title: Court Jails 75-year-Old Man For Defiling Minor
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Rivers State Family Court sitting in Port Harcourt has convicted and sentenced a 75-year-old man, Promise Chukwumatti, to 10 years’ imprisonment without option of fine for defiling an 11-year-old girl. The court also ordered that the convict to pay N1 million to the family of the minor as compensati

item.title: NSE Stock Draws Rare ‘Sell’ Call Ahead of Long-Awaited IPO Debut
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: National Stock Exchange of India Ltd., the operator of the world’s largest derivatives exchange by trading volume, has received a ‘sell’ recommendation just as it gears up to launch the country’s biggest initial public offering.

item.title: Senate Gives NNPC Auditors 1 Week To Explain N210trn Financial Entries
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Senate Committee on Public Accounts has given the external auditors of the Nigerian National Petroleum Company (NNPC) Limited a one-week deadline to provide a detailed breakdown of over N210 trillion recorded as receivables and payables in the company’s audited financial statements. The ultimatu

item.title: Guinea: Can a raw gold export ban build a refining industry?
item.dc:creator: The Africa Report
item.description: Conakry wants to stop exporting raw gold and start refining it at home. The plan could keep more wealth in Guinea, but only if the country can build the industry to match its ambition.

item.title: Wheat Eases as Traders Lock In Gains, Assess Black Sea Threats
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Wheat extended a decline as traders took profits, against a backdrop of Black Sea supply risks and weak US export demand.

item.title: Key takeaways from Donald Trump’s controversial speech on election security
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: The president claimed China and 'deep state' actors had deceived US voters, but critics say his claims were misleading.

item.title: Presidential Aide Urges Monarchs To Support Security Agencies, Tinubu’s Re-election
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The senior special assistant to President Bola Tinubu on Chieftaincy Affairs, Alhaji Abbas Tijjani Hassan, has appealed traditional rulers across the country to support the Nigerian leader’s relection next year. He also urged them to strengthen collaboration with security agencies to address Nigeria

item.title: US bombards Iran in sixth-straight night of attacks
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: The US has made good on threats to bomb civilian infrastructure in Iran in its sixth-straight night of attacks.

item.title: South Africa: Ramaphosa Pleads 'Humiliation' in Bid to Stop Impeachment Inquiry in Its Tracks
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [Daily Maverick] Cyril Ramaphosa claims ongoing impeachment proceedings will lead to significant humiliation, as the High Court weighs his urgent application to halt the inquiry into the Phala Phala allegations.

item.title: Drop in inflation rate not same as falling prices, relief for households —Experts
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: ECONOMIC experts have cautioned Nigerians against interpreting the recent moderation in the country’s headline inflation rate as evidence that prices of goods and services are falling, stressing that millions of households are yet to experience meaningful relief from the high cost of living. The war

item.title: South Africa: Six Refugees Beaten in Xenophobic Attack
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [GroundUp] African refugees working in salons around Durban's Warwick Street Market were attacked and beaten by men with sticks. Two of them were hospitalised.

item.title: Kenya: Greenpeace Demands Halt to Dangote's $17bn Kenya Coastal Refinery
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [RFI] Climate rights group Greenpeace Africa called for a halt to a proposed mega oil refinery project by Africa's richest man, Aliko Dangote, in Kenya's coastal region, warning of environmental risks.

item.title: South Africa: From Clinics to Public Health - Concerns Grow Over Anti-Migrant Campaign
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [Health-e] Healthcare workers, humanitarian organisations and migrants are warning that South Africa's anti-migrant campaign is beginning to disrupt access to healthcare, raising concerns about interrupted chronic health treatment and broader public health.

item.title: Ogun: Abiodun swears in 6 new permanent secretaries
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Abiodun emphasized that the appointees were expected to contribute meaningfully to the continued transformation of the State Civil/Public Service and support the delivery of effective governance for the benefit of the people

item.title: Ondo APC stakeholders protest alleged assembly candidate substitution
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: THE crisis rocking the All Progressives Congress (APC) in Ondo State over the outcome of its primary elections deepened on Thursday as party stakeholders in Akoko South-West Local Government Area protested the alleged replacement of the party’s House of Assembly candidate for Akoko South-West Consti

item.title: US launches new strikes as Iran says civilian infrastructure hit
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Tehran says the US strikes hit bridges, while the US boards a ship in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Israeli settlers attack Sinjil town in occupied West Bank
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Clashes erupted after Israeli settlers attacked the outskirts of the occupied West Bank town of Sinjil.

item.description: While addressing the prospective headteachers, Chairman of the Board, Olalekan Ifede, noted that their invitation for the screening was based on their qualifications and readiness to assume leadership roles, explaining that the exercise was designed to assess their intellectual capacity and overall

item.title: Iran-Linked Tankers U-Turn and Zig-Zag as US Enforces Blockade
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Two US-sanctioned tankers carrying cooking fuel are U-turning and zig-zagging in the Gulf of Oman and Arabian Sea, part of a flotilla of vessels laden with Iranian energy exports that are facing an aggressive US blockade of the country’s shipping.

item.title: Africa’s next unicorns will come from financial inclusion, creative economy — Investors
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: Africa’s next generation of billion-dollar companies is likely to emerge from financial inclusion, the creative economy, sports and small and read more Africa’s next unicorns will come from financial inclusion, creative economy — Investors

item.title: That gory incident in Benue
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: THE gruesome death of two young girls in Zaki-Biam, Ukum Local Government Area of Benue State, is more than just another crime statistic. It is a searing indictment of a society that has lost its moral compass. On Tuesday, July 7, the Benue State Police Public Relations Officer, Udeme Edet, confirme

item.title: Howden Plots Giant Capital Raise on Path to Blockbuster IPO
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Howden Group, the London-based insurance broker that’s chasing an initial public offering by 2030, is in talks to raise several billion pounds in new private capital as it seeks to maintain its break-neck expansion.

item.description: The United States (US) government has announced plans to limit visas for foreign journalists (I Visas) to 240 days. The read more US to limit visas for foreign journalists, Int’l students, others

item.description: • One voids commission’s descretion, the other upholds constitutional provisions Alex Enumah in Abuja An evidently disturbing development played up yes-terday in the judiciary after two Appeal Courts handed down

item.title: Shettima’s final test, by Azu Ishiekwene
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Vice President Kashim Shettima cannot be blamed for having doubts about whether President Bola Ahmed Tinubu would renominate him as his running mate for a second term. As governor of Lagos State for eight years, Tinubu used three deputies: KofoworolaBucknor-Akerele, Femi Pedro, and AbiodunOgunleye.

item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: The Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN) will continue to strengthen its supervision of banks despite the successful completion of the read more Bank recapitalisation won’t end CBN supervision, says Cardoso

item.title: The unidentified problem of Nigeria: From forced migration to AUTOSUCOM Revolution (I)
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Victor-Bandele Dada For more than six decades, Nigeria has debated its problems without ever adequately identifying the problem beneath the problems. Economists have diagnosed fiscal instability. Political scientists have examined institutional weakness. Sociologists have studied ethnic and relig

item.title: Japan LDP Seeks to Rein in ‘Excessive’ Activist Proposals
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: A Japanese ruling party panel is calling for stricter limits on shareholder rights, arguing that existing regulations encourage what it sees as “excessive” activist proposals and the pursuit of short-term profits.

item.title: Jimoh Ibrahim is uninformed: UN intervenes in nation’s internal affairs, by Owei Lakemfa
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: I do not support the call by Oyo State Governor SeyiMakinde that international bodies like the United Nations (UN) should participate in probing the 56-day abduction of 39 pupils and six teachers in the state. In receiving the freed victims, he called “on the appropriate international human rights a

item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: • Oyedele: Achieving investment grade status can save Nigeria N5.84tn •Says Africa loses over $74.5bn annually in additional borrowing costs due to perception gaps Ndubuisi Francis in Abuja The federal

item.title: Eoptolink Is Said to File for Up to $5 Billion Hong Kong Listing
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Eoptolink Technology Inc., a Chinese maker of optical transceivers, has filed confidentially for a multibillion-dollar listing in Hong Kong, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Kaduna govt reaffirms commitment to tackling malnutrition
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The commitment was made on Thursday in Zaria during a two-day quarterly coordination and review meeting of the State Committee on Food and Nutrition (SCFN) with nutrition focal persons from all 23 LGAs. item.title: Wema Bank Reinforces Role in Powering Fintech Growth Through Strategic Partner Engagement
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: Bennett Oghifo Wema Bank has reinforced its position as a key enabler of Nigeria’s fintech ecosystem through a strategic engagement with its fintech partners, bringing together founders, technology providers and

item.title: Just what is government doing for us? By Adekunle Adekoya
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: IN the last two weeks or thereabouts, I have been one very angry Nigerian. I don’t know whether there are others like me who are angry. If there are, I do not know how many of us are angry. I also don’t know where the other angry Nigerians are, apart from those who vent their anger […] The pos

item.title: Tinubu: Abuja City Walk Will Transform FCT Into Global Hub for Commerce, Innovation
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: Olawale Ajimotokan in Abuja President Bola Tinubu has said the Abuja Abuja City Walk would redefine the Federal Capital Territory as a global hub for commerce, innovation and modern urban

item.title: Soludo Praises Tinubu for Awarding Road Contracts in Anambra
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: David-Chyddy Eleke in Awka The governor of Anambra State, Professor Chukwuma Soludo, has praised President Bola Tinubu, for awarding the construction of two important roads in the state. Soludo conveyed

item.title: Why Nigeria must invest in mangroves preservation
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Nigeria and the world are bleeding biodiversity at an alarming rate. Globally, the United Nations (UN) reports that 420 million hectares of forest, which is roughly the size of the European Union, have been lost since 1990, with mangroves vanishing even faster at 35 per cent of their original extent

item.title: Senate Halts Procurement Reform, Insists on Budget Transparency First
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: • Akpabio, senators admit lawmakers lack full knowledge of National Assembly finances   •As tender board proposal withdrawn Sunday Aborisade in Abuja The Senate on Thursday shelved a proposal to establish

item.title: Trump alleges 'shocking vulnerabilities' in US election security ahead of midterms
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: The president accuses China of meddling in the 2020 election, directly contradicting past findings from US intelligence.

item.title: Appeal Court voids key Electoral Act provisions on parties’ primary election, membership register
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The Court of Appeal held that the disputed sections of the Electoral Act conflicted with sections 221 and 222 of the Nigerian constitution. The post Appeal Court voids key Electoral Act provisions on parties’ primary election, membership register appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: Nigerians want cheaper petrol, but renewed Hormuz battle won’t make that happen
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: Many Nigerians clamoured that petrol prices should return to the pre-war level. The post Nigerians want cheaper petrol, but renewed Hormuz battle won’t make that happen appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: Why Tinubu must lead football governance reforms – Experts/Stakeholders
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The bad news for President Tinubu is that while other nations are harvesting the goodwill, unity and good vibes from the World Cup, Nigerians continue to grumble in anger, tearing at each other because the singular, most unifying thing that brings Nigerians of all creed together (football) is not ge

item.title: Imo: Presidency hails Uzodimma on landmark infrastructure development
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Governor Uzodimma was applauded for driving an infrastructure revolution that is transforming the state into a major hub for innovation, healthcare, education and economic development.

item.title: The virtues of fulfilling promises in Islam
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Wada is called ‘Al-Ahdu’ in Arabic. Al-Ahdu literally means promise, an undertaking, a pledge, agreement, or to give words to someone, or to commit oneself for something. In the Islamic terminology, Wada means to fulfil properly a promise or pledge already agreed upon. In the first verse of Surah al

item.title: EM Stocks Headed For Weekly Loss on Tech Selloff, Rising Crude
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Emerging-market equities fell for a second day and are headed for a weekly decline as concerns over stretched AI valuations dragged technology shares lower, triggering a selloff across Asia.
